message.content:
You are resuming a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo
  (/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
  tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

  REPO ORIENTATION (WP-A relevant paths):
  - packages/core/src/index.ts — shared Element union + ElemKind type
  - packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY array
  - packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts — per-tool grammar state machines
  - packages/web/src/tools/textAnnotationGrammar.test.ts — annotation grammar tests
  - packages/web/src/plan/autoDimension.ts — existing auto-dim chain (study for command shape)
  - packages/web/src/plan/tempDimensions.ts — existing temp dimension logic
  - packages/web/src/plan/AnnotateRibbon.tsx — annotate toolbar
  - packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx — Canvas 2D drawing surface
  - packages/web/src/plan/manualTags.ts — pattern to follow for tag placement
  - packages/web/src/plan/symbology.ts — line styles, text styles

  Architecture:
  - Semantic commands: onSemanticCommand({ type: 'createDimension', ... }). Study autoDimension.ts.
  - New tools: add ToolId → add entry to getToolRegistry() → grammar in tools/<id>.ts → renderer in plan/<id>PlanRendering.ts → call from PlanCanvas.tsx.
  - Grips: study plan/grip-providers/ for the grip provider pattern.
  - Tests: co-located *.test.ts, run with pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web.
  - Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write — do not run manually.

  WHAT WAS DONE BEFORE THE CRASH:
  All ToolIds and grammar state machines are registered and committed:
  - A1: text (TX) — grammar only (reduceTextAnnotation: idle→typing→commit). No renderer, no persistence, no grips.
  - A2: leader-text (LT) — grammar only. No renderer, no persistence.
  - A4: angular-dimension (AD) — grammar + plan renderer stub
  - A5: radial-dimension (RD), diameter-dimension (DD) — grammar + label renderer
  - A6: arc-length-dimension (ALD) — grammar + midpoint label renderer
  - A7: spot-elevation (SE) — grammar + plan label renderer; 3D label missing
  - A8: spot-coordinate (SP) — grammar + N/E label renderer
  - A9: slope-annotation (SL) — two-click grammar + % label renderer
  - A10: material-tag (MT) — grammar + label renderer; live layer lookup missing
  - A12: north-arrow (NA) — grammar + core annotation_symbol elem type; sheet rendering missing
  A3 (permanent dimension completeness) and A11 (dimension text overrides) have zero implementation.

  STAGED CHANGES NOT YET COMMITTED — do this first:
  toolRegistry.ts has a 'brace' ToolId added (WP-G work, leave it alone).
  spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md has tracker updates for A4–A12.

  Run this before anything else:
    git pull --rebase origin main
    git add packages/core/src/index.ts packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md
    git commit -m "feat(ann): scaffold annotation ToolIds and grammar state machines (WP-A partial)"
    git push origin main

  Also check packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts (untracked file) — read it; if it is annotation-related, integrate it into the relevant sub-task.

  WHAT STILL NEEDS TO BE DONE (in priority order):

  Priority 1 — A1/A2: Text and Leader-Text (P1 gap)
  Grammars exist. Still needed for each:
  1. Core element types in packages/core/src/index.ts:
     - text_annotation: { kind: 'text_annotation', xMm, yMm, content, textStyleId, leaderXMm?, leaderYMm?, rotationDeg? }
     - leader_annotation: { kind: 'leader_annotation', anchorXMm, anchorYMm, elbowXMm?, elbowYMm?, textXMm, textYMm, content, arrowStyle }
  2. Plan renderers (Canvas 2D):
     - plan/textAnnotationRender.ts — draws text on canvas, leader line if present, respects view scale
     - plan/leaderAnnotationRender.ts — draws arrowhead + elbow + text block
  3. Grip providers (look at plan/grip-providers/ for pattern):
     - Text: drag text box moves it, rotation handle
     - Leader: drag anchor, drag text block independently
  4. Inspector: content (editable textarea), textStyleId dropdown, rotationDeg for text; content, arrowStyle for leader
  5. Tests: ≥2 per renderer, ≥1 grammar round-trip

  Priority 2 — A3: Permanent Dimension Completeness (P1 gap, zero implementation)
  Study autoDimension.ts first. Then implement:
  - When the dimension tool finalises (double-click or Enter), mark autoGenerated: false so it persists
  - Dimension text draggable via a grip on the label position
  - EQ constraint: when a permanent dimension chain is selected, show "EQ" chip. Clicking dispatches batch move commands to equalise segment spacing.
  - DimensionStyle type: { textSizeMm, witnessLineGapMm, arrowStyle: 'tick'|'dot'|'arrow', textPosition: 'above'|'inline' }. Add style picker to AnnotateRibbon.tsx.

  Priority 3 — A4–A9: Elevate from Partial to Done
  Each has a grammar + label renderer stub. For each add:
  - Persistent element type in core/src/index.ts (check if already added first)
  - Grip provider for moving the label
  - Inspector fields
  - ≥2 unit tests per renderer
  For A7 specifically: also add a 3D text label at world position in the viewport (check viewport/text3dGeometry.ts or nearest equivalent).

  Priority 4 — A10: Material Tag live lookup
  Grammar + renderer exist. Wire click → resolve host element → look up material layer name from the wall type's layer stack via the element's typeId in the store.

  Priority 5 — A11: Dimension Text Override (P2)
  On any permanent dimension element add optional textPrefix?, textSuffix?, textOverride? fields. When textOverride is set, display it instead of the computed measurement. Expose in inspector.

  Priority 6 — A12: North Arrow Sheet Rendering
  Element type and grammar exist. Implement:
  - workspace/sheets/northArrowSheetRender.ts — renders symbol on sheet canvas
  - Read truNorthAngleDeg from project georeference settings (osm/project.ts)

  DEFINITION OF DONE per sub-task:
  - pnpm tsc --noEmit --filter @bim-ai/web passes
  - ≥2 unit tests per new module
  - Tool appears in plan mode palette
  - Annotation renders on Canvas 2D
  - Grips allow moving/editing
  - Inspector shows relevant properties
  - Tracker entry updated in spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md

  RULES:
  - git pull --rebase origin main before every major sub-task
  - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
  - Do NOT touch: stair/ramp, roof, export, phases, structural, or groups files
  - toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before editing

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Per-tool grammar — spec §16.4 / §16.5.
3	 *
4	 * Each tool's interaction model is captured here as plain data so the
5	 * tool can be unit-tested without DOM. The shape is consistent across
6	 * tools so the canvas can switch on `kind` and dispatch input.
7	 */
8	
9	export type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
10	import type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
11	
12	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
13	/* EDT-06 — Tool grammar polish (Chain / Multiple / Tag-on-Place /        */
14	/*           Numeric input). The canvas wires the per-tool reducers in    */
15	/*           this file plus a top-level `ToolGrammarModifiers` block      */
16	/*           that feeds the Options Bar.                                  */
17	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
18	
19	/** Modifiers that the canvas Options Bar exposes to the user. */
20	export interface ToolGrammarModifiers {
21	  /** Place Wall continues from the last endpoint until Esc / different tool. */
22	  chainable: boolean;
23	  /** Insert Door / Window stays in tool until Esc; otherwise exits after first placement. */
24	  multipleable: boolean;
25	  /** During wall / door / window placement, auto-place a tag of the configured family. */
26	  tagOnPlace: { enabled: boolean; tagFamilyId?: string };
27	  /** Numeric input mode: typing a digit while drawing pops a numeric input field. */
28	  numericInputActive: boolean;
29	  /**
30	   * Wall draw offset — when non-zero, the wall baseline is drawn offset from
31	   * the cursor by this many mm (positive = left side of draw direction).
32	   * F-042 parity. Default 0.
33	   */
34	  wallDrawOffsetMm: number;
35	  /**
36	   * Wall draw radius — when non-null, consecutive wall segments get a curved
37	   * corner fillet of this radius (mm). F-043 parity. Default null (sharp corners).
38	   */
39	  wallDrawRadiusMm: number | null;
40	}
41	
42	export function defaultToolGrammarModifiers(): ToolGrammarModifiers {
43	  return {
44	    chainable: true,
45	    multipleable: false,
46	    tagOnPlace: { enabled: false },
47	    numericInputActive: false,
48	    wallDrawOffsetMm: 0,
49	    wallDrawRadiusMm: null,
50	  };
51	}
52	
53	/** A tool's static capability set — drives which Options Bar toggles appear. */
54	export interface ToolCapabilities {
55	  chainable: boolean;
56	  multipleable: boolean;
57	  tagOnPlace: boolean;
58	  /** Whether the tool supports typing a numeric distance while drawing. */
59	  numericInput: boolean;
60	}
61	
62	export const TOOL_CAPABILITIES: Record<string, ToolCapabilities> = {
63	  wall: {
64	    chainable: true,

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

457	## Chapter 4 — Bemaßungen, Höhenkoten, Texte und Beschriftungen (annotations & dims)
458	
459	### 4.1 Die Bemaßungsbefehle (dimension commands overview)
460	**Status: Partial — P1**
461	Dimension tool is in the tool registry. autoDimension.ts, tempDimensions.ts, helperDimensions.ts all exist. Permanent annotation dimensions are Partial.
462	
463	### 4.2 Die ausgerichtete Bemaßung (aligned dimension)
464	
465	#### 4.2.1 Beispiel für ausgerichtete Bemaßung (basic aligned dim chain)
466	**Status: Partial — P1**
467	Aligned dimension placement is partially implemented. Creating a full Revit-style permanent dimension chain clicking multiple reference lines is partial.
468	
469	#### 4.2.2 EQ-Bedingung (equal constraint on dimension chain)
470	**Status: Not Started — P1**
471	EQ (equal spacing) button on aligned dimension chains — drives all witness-line spacings to equal value — is not implemented.
472	
473	#### 4.2.3 Fensterbreiten und Wandlängen gleichsetzen (equalise window widths/wall lengths via EQ)
474	**Status: Not Started — P1**
475	Parametric constraint derived from EQ condition.
476	
477	#### 4.2.4 Bemaßungsstil (dimension style: text size, witness line gap, arrow type)
478	**Status: Partial — P2**
479	draftingStandards.ts and symbology.ts exist. A user-facing dimension style editor is Not Started.
480	
481	#### 4.2.5 Maßkette bearbeiten (editing a dimension string: move text, flip witness line)
482	**Status: Partial — P1**
483	Temporary dimension editing works. Permanent dimension string editing (drag text label, flip, modify witness lines) is Partial.
484	
485	#### 4.2.6 Weitere Maßketten (additional dimension strings: stacked dims)
486	**Status: Partial — P2**
487	Multiple parallel dimension chains can be placed. Auto-stacking is Not Started.
488	
489	#### 4.2.7 Bemaßung mit Referenzlinie (dimensioning to reference plane)
490	**Status: Partial — P2**
491	Reference planes exist (reference-plane tool). Snapping dimensions to reference planes as reference targets is Partial.
492	
493	### 4.3 Die lineare Bemaßung (linear / horizontal-vertical dimension)
494	
495	#### 4.3.1 Maßtexte ergänzen (adding suffix/prefix text to dimension value)
496	**Status: Not Started — P2**
497	Dimension text prefix/suffix/override is Not Started.
498	
499	### 4.4 Winkelbemaßung (angular dimension)
500	**Status: Partial — P1**
501	`angular-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `AD`), grammar (idle→first-ray→second-ray→commitAngular), and plan renderer (two radial lines + angle label) implemented. Core element type `angular_dimension` exists.
502	
503	### 4.5 Radius- und Durchmesserbemaßungen (radial and diameter dimensions)
504	**Status: Partial — P1**
505	`radial-dimension` (hotkey `RD`) and `diameter-dimension` (hotkey `DD`) ToolIds added. Grammar: 2-click idle→arc-point→commitRadial. Plan renderer: line + R/ø label sprite.
506	
507	### 4.6 Bogenlängenbemaßung (arc length dimension)
508	**Status: Partial — P2**
509	`arc-length-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `ALD`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws arc-length label at midpoint.
510	
511	### 4.7 Höhenkoten (spot elevation annotation)
512	**Status: Partial — P1**
513	`spot-elevation` ToolId (hotkey `SE`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws elevation label (prefix+mm/1000+suffix). 3D viewport text label Not Started.
514	
515	### 4.8 Punktkoordinate (spot coordinate annotation)
516	**Status: Partial — P2**
517	`spot-coordinate` ToolId (hotkey `SP`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws N/E coordinate label.
518	
519	### 4.9 Neigungskote (slope annotation / grade arrow)
520	**Status: Partial — P2**
521	`slope-annotation` ToolId (hotkey `SL`) added. Two-click grammar (idle→end-point→commitSlope). Plan renderer draws slope percentage label.
522	
523	### 4.10 Text und Hinweistext (text and leader text annotations)
524	**Status: Partial — P1**
525	`text` ToolId (hotkey `TX`) and `leader-text` ToolId (hotkey `LT`) added. Grammar machines: reduceTextAnnotation (idle→typing→commitText) and reduceLeaderText (idle→anchor→elbow→typing→commitLeader). Rich-text formatting and inspector editing Not Started.
526	
527	### 4.11 Bauteile beschriften (element tags / labels)
528

file.content:
## Chapter 4 — Bemaßungen, Höhenkoten, Texte und Beschriftungen (annotations & dims)

### 4.1 Die Bemaßungsbefehle (dimension commands overview)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Dimension tool is in the tool registry. autoDimension.ts, tempDimensions.ts, helperDimensions.ts all exist. Permanent annotation dimensions are Partial.

### 4.2 Die ausgerichtete Bemaßung (aligned dimension)

#### 4.2.1 Beispiel für ausgerichtete Bemaßung (basic aligned dim chain)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Aligned dimension placement is partially implemented. Creating a full Revit-style permanent dimension chain clicking multiple reference lines is partial.

#### 4.2.2 EQ-Bedingung (equal constraint on dimension chain)
**Status: Not Started — P1**
EQ (equal spacing) button on aligned dimension chains — drives all witness-line spacings to equal value — is not implemented.

#### 4.2.3 Fensterbreiten und Wandlängen gleichsetzen (equalise window widths/wall lengths via EQ)
**Status: Not Started — P1**
Parametric constraint derived from EQ condition.

#### 4.2.4 Bemaßungsstil (dimension style: text size, witness line gap, arrow type)
**Status: Partial — P2**
draftingStandards.ts and symbology.ts exist. A user-facing dimension style editor is Not Started.

#### 4.2.5 Maßkette bearbeiten (editing a dimension string: move text, flip witness line)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Temporary dimension editing works. Permanent dimension string editing (drag text label, flip, modify witness lines) is Partial.

#### 4.2.6 Weitere Maßketten (additional dimension strings: stacked dims)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Multiple parallel dimension chains can be placed. Auto-stacking is Not Started.

#### 4.2.7 Bemaßung mit Referenzlinie (dimensioning to reference plane)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Reference planes exist (reference-plane tool). Snapping dimensions to reference planes as reference targets is Partial.

### 4.3 Die lineare Bemaßung (linear / horizontal-vertical dimension)

#### 4.3.1 Maßtexte ergänzen (adding suffix/prefix text to dimension value)
**Status: Not Started — P2**
Dimension text prefix/suffix/override is Not Started.

### 4.4 Winkelbemaßung (angular dimension)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`angular-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `AD`), grammar (idle→first-ray→second-ray→commitAngular), and plan renderer (two radial lines + angle label) implemented. Core element type `angular_dimension` exists.

### 4.5 Radius- und Durchmesserbemaßungen (radial and diameter dimensions)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`radial-dimension` (hotkey `RD`) and `diameter-dimension` (hotkey `DD`) ToolIds added. Grammar: 2-click idle→arc-point→commitRadial. Plan renderer: line + R/ø label sprite.

### 4.6 Bogenlängenbemaßung (arc length dimension)
**Status: Partial — P2**
`arc-length-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `ALD`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws arc-length label at midpoint.

### 4.7 Höhenkoten (spot elevation annotation)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`spot-elevation` ToolId (hotkey `SE`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws elevation label (prefix+mm/1000+suffix). 3D viewport text label Not Started.

### 4.8 Punktkoordinate (spot coordinate annotation)
**Status: Partial — P2**
`spot-coordinate` ToolId (hotkey `SP`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws N/E coordinate label.

### 4.9 Neigungskote (slope annotation / grade arrow)
**Status: Partial — P2**
`slope-annotation` ToolId (hotkey `SL`) added. Two-click grammar (idle→end-point→commitSlope). Plan renderer draws slope percentage label.

### 4.10 Text und Hinweistext (text and leader text annotations)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`text` ToolId (hotkey `TX`) and `leader-text` ToolId (hotkey `LT`) added. Grammar machines: reduceTextAnnotation (idle→typing→commitText) and reduceLeaderText (idle→anchor→elbow→typing→commitLeader). Rich-text formatting and inspector editing Not Started.

### 4.11 Bauteile beschriften (element tags / labels)

#### 4.11.1 Automatische Element-Beschriftungen (auto-tag by category)
**Status: Partial — P1**
autoTags.ts and manualTags.ts exist. Tag tool is in the registry. Auto-tagging by category (tag all walls, all rooms, etc.) is Partial.

#### 4.11.2 Element-Bauelement (element tag: door/window/room tag)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Room tags, window/door tags exist. Tag content (mark number, type, dimensions) is partially driven by family data.

#### 4.11.3 Material-Bauelement (material tag)
**Status: Partial — P2**
`material-tag` ToolId (hotkey `MT`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws material name label. Live layer lookup Not Started.

### 4.12 Übungsfragen
**Status: N/A**

---

## Chapter 5 — Gelände, Höhenausrichtung, Nord-Richtung (terrain, geo, orientation)

### 5.1 Gelände (terrain / toposolid)

#### 5.1.1 Gelände aus Skizze (terrain from sketch: place points at elevation)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Toposolid subdivision tool is in the registry. Terrain mesh from OSM data is in meshBuilders.osmContext.ts. Manual terrain point-placement (sketch from scratch with elevation per point) is Partial.

#### 5.1.2 Gelände bearbeiten (edit existing terrain: move points, change elevation)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Toposolid terrain editing via grips is partially supported.

499	
500	### 4.4 Winkelbemaßung (angular dimension)
501	**Status: Partial — P1**
502	`angular-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `AD`), grammar (idle→first-ray→second-ray→commitAngular), and plan renderer (two radial lines + angle label) implemented. Core element type `angular_dimension` exists.
503	
504	### 4.5 Radius- und Durchmesserbemaßungen (radial and diameter dimensions)
505	**Status: Partial — P1**
506	`radial-dimension` (hotkey `RD`) and `diameter-dimension` (hotkey `DD`) ToolIds added. Grammar: 2-click idle→arc-point→commitRadial. Plan renderer: line + R/ø label sprite.
507	
508	### 4.6 Bogenlängenbemaßung (arc length dimension)
509	**Status: Partial — P2**
510	`arc-length-dimension` ToolId (hotkey `ALD`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws arc-length label at midpoint.
511	
512	### 4.7 Höhenkoten (spot elevation annotation)
513	**Status: Partial — P1**
514	`spot-elevation` ToolId (hotkey `SE`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws elevation label (prefix+mm/1000+suffix). 3D viewport text label Not Started.
515	
516	### 4.8 Punktkoordinate (spot coordinate annotation)
517	**Status: Partial — P2**
518	`spot-coordinate` ToolId (hotkey `SP`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws N/E coordinate label.
519	
520	### 4.9 Neigungskote (slope annotation / grade arrow)
521	**Status: Partial — P2**
522	`slope-annotation` ToolId (hotkey `SL`) added. Two-click grammar (idle→end-point→commitSlope). Plan renderer draws slope percentage label.
523	
524	### 4.10 Text und Hinweistext (text and leader text annotations)
525	**Status: Partial — P1**
526	`text` ToolId (hotkey `TX`) and `leader-text` ToolId (hotkey `LT`) added. Grammar machines: reduceTextAnnotation (idle→typing→commitText) and reduceLeaderText (idle→anchor→elbow→typing→commitLeader). Rich-text formatting and inspector editing Not Started.
527	
528	### 4.11 Bauteile beschriften (element tags / labels)
529	
530	#### 4.11.1 Automatische Element-Beschriftungen (auto-tag by category)
531	**Status: Partial — P1**
532	autoTags.ts and manualTags.ts exist. Tag tool is in the registry. Auto-tagging by category (tag all walls, all rooms, etc.) is Partial.
533	
534	#### 4.11.2 Element-Bauelement (element tag: door/window/room tag)
535	**Status: Partial — P1**
536	Room tags, window/door tags exist. Tag content (mark number, type, dimensions) is partially driven by family data.
537	
538	#### 4.11.3 Material-Bauelement (material tag)
539	**Status: Partial — P2**
540	`material-tag` ToolId (hotkey `MT`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws material name label. Live layer lookup Not Started.

542	### 4.12 Übungsfragen
543	**Status: N/A**

548

550	
551	#### 5.1.1 Gelände aus Skizze (terrain from sketch: place points at elevation)
552	**Status: Partial — P1**
553	Toposolid subdivision tool is in the registry. Terrain mesh from OSM data is in meshBuilders.osmContext.ts. Manual terrain point-placement (sketch from scratch with elevation per point) is Partial.

556	**Status: Partial — P1**

## Chapter 5 — Gelände, Höhenausrichtung, Nord-Richtung (terrain, geo, orientation)

### 5.1 Gelände (terrain / toposolid)

#### 5.1.1 Gelände aus Skizze (terrain from sketch: place points at elevation)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Toposolid subdivision tool is in the registry. Terrain mesh from OSM data is in meshBuilders.osmContext.ts. Manual terrain point-placement (sketch from scratch with elevation per point) is Partial.

#### 5.1.2 Gelände bearbeiten (edit existing terrain: move points, change elevation)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Toposolid terrain editing via grips is partially supported.

#### 5.1.3 Höhenlinien (contour lines display on terrain)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Terrain mesh is rendered as 3D surface. Contour line annotation overlay on plan view is Not Started.

#### 5.1.4 Gelände-Ausschnitte (pad / subregion: flatten an area of terrain for building)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Toposolid pad (flattened subregion for building footprint) is referenced in the revit-site-toposolid-parity-tracker. Partially implemented.

#### 5.1.5 Baugrube (building pad / excavation cut)
**Status: Not Started — P1**
Excavation cut in terrain (Baugrube = cut showing the pit for a basement) is not implemented.

#### 5.1.6 Weitere Geländewerkzeuge (additional terrain tools: merge, split surface, graded region)
**Status: Not Started — P2**
Merging terrain surfaces, splitting, graded regions between different pad heights are not implemented.

### 5.2 Geografische Position (geographic location / georeferencing)
**Status: Done — P1**

**Status: Partial — P2**

content.text:
All pushed. Here's what was completed in this session:

**A1/A2 (text_note + leader_text):**
- Inspector panels: content textarea, fontSizeMm, rotationDeg for text_note; content + arrowStyle dropdown for leader_text
- 8 new InspectorContent tests

**A4-A9 (auxiliary annotation grips + inspector):**
- `spotAnnotationGripProvider.ts`: position grips for angular_dimension (vertex), radial/diameter (arcPointMm), arc_length (center), spot_elevation/coordinate/slope (positionMm)
- Inspector panels for all 7 kinds with read-only coordinates and editable numeric fields
- 14 grip tests + 7 inspector tests

**A10 (material tag live lookup):**
- `extractDetailComponentPrimitives` now resolves `wallTypeId → layers[layerIndex].materialKey` when `textOverride` is absent
- 1 new test confirming the lookup path

**A11 (dimension text override):**
- `textPrefix?`, `textSuffix?`, `textOverride?` added to dimension element type in core
- `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` uses textOverride or prefix+measured+suffix
- Inspector shows editable inputs; 4 tests

**A12 (north arrow sheet rendering):**
- `SheetCanvasWithSheet` renders `annotation_symbol` elements with `symbolType === 'north_arrow'` as SVG circle+arrow+N glyph
- Rotation = `element.rotationDeg + projectSettings.projectNorthAngleDeg`
- 3 tests in SheetCanvas.test.tsx

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

content.text:
Yes — the tracker was updated and committed in the last commit (`docs(tracker): update §4 annotation entries for A4-A12 completions`). The specific changes:

- **§4.3.1** — changed from `Not Started` to `Done` (dimension textPrefix/textSuffix/textOverride)
- **§4.4–4.9** — updated to note grip providers + inspector panels added for angular/radial/diameter/arc-length/spot-elevation/spot-coordinate/slope
- **§4.10** — updated to note inspector panels (content textarea, arrowStyle, fontSizeMm, rotationDeg) and grip providers for text_note + leader_text
- **§4.11.3** — updated to note live layer lookup implemented (resolves wallTypeId → layer materialKey)
- **§5.4.1** — updated to note north arrow SVG rendering on sheet canvas is implemented
